channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canberra sport wrap: Women's rugby league sideline scuffle, Magpies return to improved home ground
item.description: A dramatic Saturday of Canberra sport saw a sideline fight break out in the women's Katrina Fanning Shield rugby league match between the Yass Magpies and Woden Valley Rams, after a hard tackle sparke...

item.title: Spirit Airlines Ceases Operations Amid Rising Fuel Costs Due to Iran War
item.description: Spirit Airlines, a US-based budget carrier, has shut down after failing to secure support from creditors for a government bailout. The company, which once accounted for 5% of US flights, was unable to...

item.title: Real Madrid Defender Carvajal Out With Toe Injury Ahead of Clasico
item.description: Real Madrid don suffer small wahala as their veteran right-back, Dani Carvajal, don fracture him toe and e go miss the big Clasico match against Barcelona wey go happen next Sunday, May 10. E fit be h...

item.title: Spirit Airlines ceases operations amid soaring jet fuel prices linked to Iran conflict
item.description: US discount carrier Spirit Airlines has officially ceased all operations on Saturday, becoming the first major casualty in the aviation industry due to the ongoing Iran war. The airline, already in a ...

item.title: AMSONS Group and Kenya partner to expand maternal healthcare with 10 new hospitals
item.description: Kenya has entered into a major agreement with Tanzania’s AMSONS Group to build 10 modern maternal and child health hospitals across nine counties, in a project valued at Tsh90.75 billion (about Ksh4.5...

item.title: Chinese Court Rules Companies Must Follow Labour Laws When Replacing Workers with AI
item.description: A Chinese court for the first time don give clear ruling wey go affect plenty people wey dey fear AI go take their jobs. The Hangzhou Intermediate People’s Court rule say companies no fit just fire wo...

item.title: Cambridge investigates AS-level maths paper leak, assures fair grading
item.description: Cambridge International Education (CIE) has confirmed that the AS Level Mathematics Paper 1 (9709) was leaked prematurely in Pakistan and other regions, violating exam regulations. The leak was discov...

item.title: Tony Blair Institute Calls for Review of Triple Lock Pension Policy
item.description: The Tony Blair Institute has urged the UK government to reconsider the current 'triple lock' pension system, describing it as outdated and financially unsustainable. The triple lock guarantees that st...

item.title: Laser Communications Enable High-Definition Data Transfer on Artemis II Mission
item.description: During the Artemis II mission, NASA successfully demonstrated the use of laser (optical) communications at lunar distances, providing high-definition video, images, and data transfer between the Orion...

item.title: Alice Springs community reacts to the tragic death of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: A devastating tragedy unfolded in Alice Springs after the body of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby was found on Thursday, following her disappearance on Sunday. The discovery came five kilometres f...

item.title: Oil block acquisition seen as a way to improve crude supply for local refineries
item.description: The report focuses on Nigeria’s effort to ease crude supply challenges facing local refineries by encouraging downstream operators to go into upstream oil block acquisition. The idea being pushed by t...

item.title: MAGA Influencers Misunderstand the Message of Animal Farm Adaptation
item.description: The latest adaptation of George Orwell's *Animal Farm*, directed by Andy Serkis, has sparked confusion among conservative influencers. Although the novel is widely recognized as a critique of totalita...

item.title: Nagaland plans public ‘Walk for Humanity’ to promote humanitarian awareness on World Red Cross Day
item.description: The Indian Red Cross Society, Nagaland State Branch (IRCSN), is set to observe World Red Cross Day on 8 May with a public outreach initiative titled ‘Walk for Humanity’ in Kohima. The event aligns wit...

item.title: Tanzania Advocates Local Processing and Industrialisation in Mining Sector
item.description: Tanzania is urging African countries to rethink how they handle their mineral resources. Speaking at the Kenya Mining Investment Conference & Expo 2026, Tanzania's Minister of Minerals, Anthony Mavund...

item.title: Misa hails Jetour's move to begin local vehicle manufacturing from 2027
item.description: The Motor Industry Staff Association (Misa) has welcomed Jetour's announcement that it will begin producing some of its models in South Africa from 2027. The union had previously raised concerns about...

item.title: Conflict in Iran indirectly accelerates renewable energy growth, UN climate chief says
item.description: According to the United Nations climate chief, the ongoing conflict in Iran is having an unexpected impact on global energy markets, inadvertently boosting investment and development in renewable powe...

item.title: Broncos Stage Remarkable Comeback Against Roosters Despite Injuries
item.description: In a thrilling NRL clash, the Sydney Roosters dominated the first half against the Brisbane Broncos, establishing a commanding 30-0 lead by half-time. Star Roosters players such as Sam Walker, Daniel ...

item.title: US-Zambia health funding agreement delayed due to limited engagement
item.description: The United States has expressed frustration with Zambia over the stalled $1 billion health aid deal, citing a lack of engagement from Zambian officials. Outgoing US ambassador Michael Gonzales stated ...

item.title: Anti-Immigrant Protest in Johannesburg Escalates into Violence
item.description: On 30 April 2026, an anti-immigrant protest in Johannesburg organised by March and March deviated from the agreed route, resulting in violent incidents in Braamfontein and Hillbrow. Approximately a th...

item.title: Repolling Conducted in Diamond Harbour and Magrahat Paschim Constituencies During Bengal Assembly Elections 2026
item.description: The 2026 Bengal Assembly elections have witnessed repolling in two key constituencies, namely Diamond Harbour and Magrahat Paschim. This move comes after concerns were raised over the fairness and smo...
